name: p27_conic_tower_quotient_cas_suite
purpose: CAS handoff for the repeated conic-chain Kummer selector tower. This is the conic-lane source/quotient test that could beat sqrt(p) if it produces a low-genus quotient, recurrence, or direct sampler.
entry_condition: Use after the raw conic-chain sampler, sign-word buckets, and low-degree two-step Kummer coordinate screens are negative. The legal pullback/quotient is the remaining sqrt-beating conic path.

tasks.name: Depth-1 legal pullback quotient
tasks.construct: legal label-2/compactD source + one conic transition
compute: component count after saturation by nonzero denominators
quotient maps under h0/g0/r1 sign symmetries
tasks.promote_if: low-genus quotient or explicit sampler of legal d3-plus rows
tasks.kill_if: only high-genus/generic components and no sourceable quotient

tasks.name: Depth-2 repeated selector quotient
tasks.construct: depth-1 legal pullback + second conic transition, equivalently add the repeated selector layer Z0/S1
compute: whether the repeated divisor Z0^2=-(L0+a0)(L0-a0)c*r1 reduces the normalized tower
dimension/components/genus after quotienting obvious sign symmetries
whether d5 class is a pullback/reuse of the d4 class
tasks.promote_if: d5 is coupled to d4 by a low-genus quotient, recurrence, or finite source walk
tasks.kill_if: d5 is a fresh independent half-cover on every low-genus quotient

tasks.name: Legal tower sampler test
tasks.construct: any quotient/source map found in priorities 10 or 20
tasks.promote_if: source-normalized prefix survivors beat independent half-loss by >=1.25x with heldout validation
tasks.kill_if: sampler pays constant/q legal-incidence denominator or reproduces raw half-loss

do_not_run: low-degree pair/triple coordinate scans already killed
GPU production from fixed conic prefixes without source-normalized lift

acceptance.promote: A low-genus/sourceable quotient, reusable Kummer class, or direct legal tower sampler that compounds beyond constant-factor filtering.
acceptance.kill: The legal pullback remains high-genus/generic after quotienting obvious symmetries and the d4/d5 classes stay independent.
